summary: Frontend regression for the big case-study refactor: replaced the inline-script + dangerouslySetInnerHTML implementation of /case-studies (library) and /case-studies/defense-aerospace-otd (detail + PDF) with proper React components reading from a single canonical data file (/app/frontend/src/data/caseStudies.js). All 8 review checkpoints PASS end-to-end. Architecture verified by reading data file + components: every consumer (CaseStudyCard.resultSummary, CaseStudyHero.statTiles, CaseStudyPrintDoc.statTiles) reads from the same dataset entry — confirmed there is no second source of truth. Zero pageerrors and zero console errors across every flow tested.
critical_code_review_comments: Single-source-of-truth architecture is clean: caseStudies.js exports the 68-entry array plus a getCaseStudy(slug) helper. Library page imports caseStudies directly; detail page imports getCaseStudy. CaseStudyHero, CaseStudyBody, and CaseStudyPrintDoc all receive the same `data` prop and read the same statTiles/diagnosisItems/results arrays — no parallel hardcoded copies anywhere.

CaseStudyCard correctly branches on `internalRoute` vs `externalUrl`: internal -> React Router <Link> (no target attr, no full reload), external -> <a target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer'>. Distribution validated at runtime: 1 internal (#54) + 67 external thepowerscompany.com links, total 68.

Schema in caseStudies.js explicitly mirrors WPGraphQL/ACF repeater shape (statTiles, diagnosisItems, results, serviceLines, challenges) — the documented migration path to Faust.js is realistic: replace the static export with a WPGraphQL query that returns the same JSON shape and the React tree continues to render unchanged.

Print emulation correctness verified via page.emulate_media('print'): site-header (display:none) and site-footer (display:none) are hidden, .print-doc becomes visible, exactly 2 .print-page children render, page 1 contains 'Results at a Glance' + Situation, page 2 contains Diagnosis (6 .pp-diag items) + Full Result (6 .pp-result items), both pages have footer with phone/address.
Tabler icons webfont (@tabler/icons-webfont@3.34.0) is loaded as a real <link rel='stylesheet'> in document.head and the CDN responds HTTP 200 — hero stat-tile and result-card icons render correctly.

Minor (not a bug, just an observation): the detail page still loads the screen-only sections in DOM even while @media print is active (they are display:none'd by CSS rather than removed from the tree). This is the correct trade-off for a SPA — avoids re-mount churn when the print dialog opens. The H2 list query returned both screen and print H2s for that reason.
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Iteration_3 PASSED all 8 review checkpoints with 0 pageerrors and 0 console errors. Detailed evidence: (1) LIBRARY /case-studies — 68
rendered. Filter dropdowns: industryFilter=15 options (incl 'All Industries'), engagementFilter=11 (incl 'All Service Types'), challengeFilter=11 (incl 'All Challenges'), sortFilter=4. Search 'defense' filters live to 1 card. Clear All button resets to 68. NO remaining inline-script + dangerouslySetInnerHTML pattern (legacyPage hook is gone for this route). (2) ANCHOR DISTRIBUTION — exactly 1 internal SPA (#54, href=/case-studies/defense-aerospace-otd, no target attr, className=case-card-link) + 67 external (target=_blank, rel=noopener noreferrer, all thepowerscompany.com URLs). (3) SPA NAV — set window.__sentinel='PRESERVED_99' on /case-studies, clicked #54, URL became /case-studies/defense-aerospace-otd and sentinel still equals 'PRESERVED_99' → confirms React Router navigation, no full reload. (4) DETAIL HERO — eyebrow 'CASE STUDY | DEFENSE & AEROSPACE', H1 'On-time performance climbed from 56% to 89%…' (sentence-case), italic descriptor 'Make-to-Order Defense Manufacturer', 'RESULTS AT A GLANCE' label + 3 stat tiles visible (59% / 65% / 69%) with Tabler icons, gold 'DOWNLOAD PDF' button (data-testid='hero-download-pdf-btn'), Executive Brief sidebar, disciplines line 'MOS · FRONTLINE LEADERSHIP · SUPPLY CHAIN'. (5) DETAIL BODY — 6 .cs-diag items + 6 .cs-result items rendered; H2s for Situation/Diagnosis/POWERS/Full Result all present; bottom CTA has 'Start a Conversation' +
rca of the issue: No issues to RCA. The refactor cleanly replaces the legacy inline-script approach (which previously caused the StrictMode `Identifier DATA has already been declared` issue tracked in iteration_1) with idiomatic React components driven by a single canonical data file. Because the page is now a normal React tree, the entire LegacyPage / IIFE-wrapping / clearFilter-hoisting workaround is no longer required for /case-studies or /case-studies/defense-aerospace-otd — that whole class of bugs is structurally eliminated for these routes.
